ATLAS Handover Guide · v2.0 Your Company · IT Operations
3
What is the Handover Form (BAST)?
Berita Acara Serah Terima · Official Asset Transfer Record
BAST is the official digital record in ATLAS for any asset that moves between IT Operations and an employee. Both sides must sign. A PDF is generated automatically.
👤
Who is involved — Employee name, NIK, department
💻
Assets involved — Item type, SKU, Asset Tag / Barcode
📍
Asset location — Site and floor assigned to the asset
✍️
Digital signatures — From both the Employee and IT Operations
📄
PDF document — Auto-generated, always accessible from the Handover List
✅
No printing needed. Both parties sign electronically, at different times, from wherever they are.
How It Works
🖥️
IT Operations
Manages & signs documents
↓ Check Out (CO)
↑ Check In (CI)
↕ Changes (CH)
👤
Employee
Receives & signs documents
📄
PDF generated automatically
ATLAS Handover Guide · v2.0 Section 1
4
Step 1: Pick Your Handover Mode
Pick this first — the form is locked until you choose
📋
Standard BAST
The default mode. Works for all standard Check Out, Check In, and asset swaps — regardless of work setup.
Use this when
✓  Issuing a new laptop or device
✓  Returning an asset
✓  Swapping broken equipment
CO- CI- CH-
🏠
WFH Mode
For employees transitioning to or from a Work-From-Home setup. Document suffix is added to track the WFH context.
Use this when
✓  Employee is going remote
✓  Issuing assets for WFH setup
✓  WFH-related returns
CO-...-WFH CI-...-WFH
🏢
WFO Mode
For employees returning to the office after WFH. Document suffix tracks the WFO transition context.
Use this when
✓  Employee is returning to office
✓  Returning WFH assets
✓  Office equipment issuance
CO-...-WFO CI-...-WFO
ATLAS Handover Guide · v2.0 Section 2
5
Step 2: Select Transaction Type
Choose after picking the mode above
CHECK OUT 📤
IT → Employee
Asset goes from IT to the employee. Used for new issuances.
Examples
✓  New laptop issued to employee
✓  Monitor assigned to new staff
✓  Peripherals for WFH setup
Prefix: CO-
CHECK IN 📥
Employee → IT
Asset goes from the employee back to IT. Used for returns.
Examples
✓  Returning laptop on resignation
✓  Asset sent in for repair
✓  End of borrowing period
Prefix: CI-
CHANGES 🔄
Swap / Replace
One asset in, one asset out in the same transaction. Must fill both sides.
Examples
✓  Broken laptop → replacement
✓  Upgrade to newer model
✓  Accessory swap
Prefix: CH-
ATLAS Handover Guide · v2.0 Section 3
6
Step-by-Step: Filling the Form
Steps 1–7: From opening the form to adding items
Phase 1 · Open the Form
1
ATLAS Sidebar → Handover Form (BAST)
The form opens immediately. No extra button.
2
Pick a Mode Card
Standard BAST / WFH / WFO (required first step).
Phase 2 · Fill User & Type Info
3
Section 1: User Information
Enter employee name, NIK, and department.
4
Section 2: Transaction Type
Pick CHECK OUT, CHECK IN, or CHANGES.
Phase 3 · Set Location
5
Asset Location (required for CO & CH)
Set the site and floor where asset will be placed.
Phase 4 · Add Asset Items
6
Click "+ Add Item to Issue" or "+ Add Item to Return"
Each row = one asset. Add as many as needed.
Item Row Example
Item Type (SKU)
Notebook / Laptop
Asset Tag
ATINB-00123
Serial No.
Optional
Step 7: Add notes if needed (e.g. "Screen cracked, returning for repair")
💡
If there's no sticker on the asset, tick the NO TAG checkbox instead of entering a tag number.
ATLAS Handover Guide · v2.0 Section 4
7
Form Structure & Finalize
Steps 8–14: Sections overview and submitting the document
The Form Has 4 Sections
Section 1 · User Information
Employee Name  ·  NIK  ·  Department
Section 2 · Transaction Type
Check Out  ·  Check In  ·  Changes
Section 3 · Asset Details (main body)
📍 Location  ·  Item rows  ·  Asset Tags  ·  Notes
Section 4 · Signature
Draw signature  ·  Finalize  ·  Lock & Submit
⚠️
Double-check everything before clicking Finalize. After that, the document is locked and cannot be edited.
Final Steps
8
Set Asset Location — site and floor (required for CO and CH)
9
Add Item Rows — click "+ Add Item" for each asset
10
Enter Asset Tag — scan or type the barcode / tag number
11
No sticker? — check the NO TAG checkbox
12
Add Notes — asset condition, remarks, or other context
13
Draw your signature in the Section 4 signature box
14
Click Finalize — document locks and becomes On Hold
ATLAS Handover Guide · v2.0 Section 4–5
8
The Signing Process
Virtual, two-party signing — both sign at different times
📝
Draft
→
⏳
On Hold
→
✅
Completed
→
📄
PDF Ready
Scenario A: Employee Creates the Form
1
Employee fills form, signs, clicks Finalize → On Hold
2
IT Operations sees it in Handover List, clicks Blue Resume button
3
IT signs → status becomes Completed → PDF ready 📄
Scenario B: IT Creates the Form
1
IT Operations fills form, signs, finalizes → On Hold
2
Employee sees it in their Handover List → clicks Blue Resume button
3
Employee signs → status becomes Completed → PDF ready 📄
ℹ️
On Hold means one party still needs to sign. Look for the blue Resume button in the Handover List to add your signature.
✅
Completed means both parties have signed. The PDF is fully generated and available via the red icon button.

Action Buttons Guide
open_in_new
Red Icon: Open PDF
Opens the signed BAST PDF in a new browser tab. Available on any finalized document, regardless of status.
Completed + always visible
edit_note
Blue Icon: Resume
Opens the document so you can add your signature. All previously entered data is locked in.
On Hold documents only
🖱️ Row Click: Full Details
Click anywhere on a row (not the action buttons) to open the full document detail view — showing all parties, items, and signatures.
ATLAS Handover Guide · v2.0 Section 8
10
FAQ & Quick Reference
Common questions and key terms
Frequently Asked Questions
Q My document shows "On Hold". What does that mean?
It means one party hasn't signed yet. Look for the blue Resume button in your Handover List. Click it to add your signature.
Q I don't know the asset tag or barcode. What do I enter?
Check the physical sticker on the asset (tags start with prefixes like ATINB- for notebooks, ATIMT- for monitors). If there's no sticker, tick the NO TAG checkbox.
Q Someone else needs to sign for me. Can they?
Yes, but only a representative at a higher level — Supervisor, Senior Supervisor, Manager, or above. A teammate at the same level cannot sign on your behalf.
Q What do the Red and Blue icons do?
open_in_new Red: opens the signed PDF.    edit_note Blue: resume and sign an On Hold document.
Key Terms
BAST
Berita Acara Serah Terima — official digital asset transfer record
CO-
Check Out — asset issued from IT to employee
CI-
Check In — asset returned from employee to IT
CH-
Changes — swap: one asset in, one asset out
NIK
Employee ID number (Nomor Induk Karyawan)
On Hold
One or both parties haven't signed yet
Completed
Both parties signed — PDF is ready
NO TAG
Asset has no barcode sticker — tick the checkbox instead
